title: common: support mounts.conf.d/ drop-in configs
body:
The base `mounts.conf` currently has a single entry that's RHEL-specific, and since there's no provision for drop-in support for mounts.conf, non-RHEL distributions need to resort to overrides to skip this mount config. Even though the mount is a no-op in case the path doesn't exist, shipping that config is misleading.

There's already [work](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/795) going on to move RHEL/distro-specific config as drop-in configs and have a distro-agnostic vanilla config shipped as default. This request follows the same idea. There's no rush for this to be a part of 6.0, though.

comments.body:
I guess one simple thing we should do is to provide an empty mounts.conf as default instead. And have the RHEL one as part of the rpm dir?

Overall I wonder how much of mounts.conf is actually a relict of the past, technically just having a field in containers.conf these days would be much better. And well we already have fields for volume mounts.
That sounds better then extending mounts.conf IMO. Though I think there is use for it in cri-o which does not use containers.conf so they likely want to keep in and then it make sense to have it shared

> I guess one simple thing we should do is to provide an empty mounts.conf as default instead. And have the RHEL one as part of the rpm dir?

Yes, that'd be a quick-win. It makes packaging a bit easier.

> That sounds better then extending mounts.conf IMO. Though I think there is use for it in cri-o which does not use containers.conf so they likely want to keep in and then it make sense to have it shared

Can't comment on the provenance of `mounts.conf` but I agree with merging this into containers.conf, for starters, having drop-ins makes things cleaner (I can have XY-<vendor>-mounts.conf). That being said, I'm not aware of how cri-o manages configurations. Can we make them use containers.conf if and when we decide to do this?

> > I guess one simple thing we should do is to provide an empty mounts.conf as default instead. And have the RHEL one as part of the rpm dir?
> 
> Yes, that'd be a quick-win. It makes packaging a bit easier.

Feel free to open a PR for that.

> Can we make them use containers.conf if and when we decide to do this?

I guess that would be an anti goal for them. There are so much podman specifics in there that exposing the entire file as it makes little sense in the crio context.

> > Can we make them use containers.conf if and when we decide to do this?
>
> I guess that would be an anti goal for them. There are so much podman specifics in there that exposing the entire file as it makes little sense in the crio context.

Thanks for the context, though I still have a rudimentary understanding of crio<>podman so far, I'll try to spend some more time to understand this better.
